data.narrative: Student demonstrated a total shift from narrative to top-down structure. Their migration plan rewrite reduced word count by 47% while significantly improving scannability and accountability through the use of tables and active voice.

dimension_scores.observation: Identified all 6 clarity problems in the legacy document, including the exact word count to reach the buried lede.
Rewrite was exceptionally concise (~185 words) and eliminated all noise phrases.
dimension_scores.evidence: We are migrating our API from REST to GraphQL over 8 weeks, starting [date].

dimension_scores.observation: Correctly defined all acronyms for a junior audience.
Used H1/H2 hierarchy and tables to ensure information was discoverable.
dimension_scores.evidence: Used a Week/Owner/Milestone table for the timeline.

dimension_scores.observation: Assigned specific owners to risk mitigation.
dimension_scores.evidence: the SRE team adds query depth limits

strengths: Exceptional structural thinking: recognized that moving the lede to the top is more effective than line-by-line editing.
data.recommendation: In Module 03, focus on the Alternatives Considered section—it is the ultimate test of tradeoff communication.

public_evidence.student_action: Rewrote a 350-word buried-lede document into a 185-word top-down execution plan with ownership tables.
public_evidence.technical_detail: Technical migration documentation structure and scannability.
public_evidence.proof: ~185 words, decision in sentence 1, scannable table for timeline.
public_evidence.ability: Advanced technical writing and structural clarity.
public_evidence.reviewer_value: Demonstrates the ability to communicate complex architectural changes (REST to GraphQL) in a high-utility, executive-friendly format.

concepts_demonstrated.evidence: Moved the GraphQL decision to the first sentence.
Found the decision 280 words deep in the original text.

teaching_approaches.detail: Compared the legacy wall-of-text to the student's scannable rewrite.
